ogTitle: Freeburn Island vs Freemans Reach: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Freeburn Island and Freemans Reach.
heading: Freeburn Island vs Freemans Reach — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Freeburn Island, New South Wales and Freemans Reach, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Freeburn Island has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Freemans Reach (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Freeburn Island or Freemans Reach safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Freeburn Island has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Freemans Reach scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Freeburn Island scores 37/100 for bushfire risk. Freemans Reach scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Freeburn Island or Freemans Reach?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Freeburn Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.78% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
